mechanism_narrative: A stabilization or pause in FEDFUNDS after a multi-year tightening cycle signals the Fed nearing terminal rate, reducing discount-rate uncertainty and supporting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS momentum and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS stabilization are not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window after a period of historic lows, it signals an inflection in labor market conditions that precedes broader economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over trailing 3 months from a cycle low
regime_hint: labor market turning point after tight-labor expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE uptick signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated FEDFUNDS combined with declining HOUST indicates the housing sector is contracting under restrictive monetary policy; this joint deterioration transmits to broader demand expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, because housing is a leading indicator of consumer wealth effects.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains above 4%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with housing cooling
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-decline/high-FEDFUNDS signal fails to produce negative median 20d forward returns relative to unconditional baseline across a 3-year walk-forward test, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth momentum predicts forward equity returns positively during expansionary monetary regimes but loses predictive power or reverses sign once FEDFUNDS exceeds a restrictive threshold, because tight monetary policy overrides real-activity signals in driving valuation.
regime_hint: accommodative-to-neutral monetary regime; effect flips in restrictive regime (FEDFUNDS>4.5%)
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to reverse or weaken when FEDFUNDS>4.5% in out-of-sample tests, or Rank IC difference between regimes is not statistically distinguishable (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strengthening consumer demand that is quickly priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as short-horizon momentum traders react to the data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive relationship (Rank IC < 0.03) with RSAFS month-over-month growth across a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO of more than 1% over two consecutive months signals an emerging industrial slowdown that leads broader corporate earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following triggered INDPRO declines are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with a still-accommodative or peaking FEDFUNDS level indicates a soft-landing scenario where labor strength does not force further tightening, jointly producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect requires both conditions since strong payrolls alone under aggressive tightening would be negative.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k while FEDFUNDS change over trailing 3 months is <= 0.25pp
regime_hint: soft-landing transition at peak of tightening cycle
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform unconditional 20d baseline return by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05) across walk-forward folds, or single-series PAYEMS model achieves equal or higher Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A peak-and-rollover in HOUST (housing starts falling more than 10% from a 12-month high) signals the end of a housing-driven demand cycle, which historically precedes broader economic deceleration and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, particularly when this occurs alongside historically low unemployment suggesting late-cycle conditions.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with low unemployment and housing rollover
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns after HOUST rollover trigger are non-negative on average across a 3-year rolling walk-forward window, or Rank IC < 0.02.
